reasoning: The general dynamic during the period extending from early November 2023 to February 2024 (shifted to 2025-2026 for this forecast) involved a significant decline in the 10-year Treasury yield from its near-5% peak. Because corporate bond yields generally adjust more slowly than Treasuries, the AAA10Y spread naturally tends to widen during such a bond rally. The fetched FRED time series definitively shows the spread widening from 1.05 in late October to 1.09 by October 22, and hitting 1.17 by November 17. The November 9 baseline would logically sit near the middle of this range (around 1.12-1.14), while the subsequent target dates (November 16, December 9, and February 7) correspond to periods when the 10-year yield had fallen further (widening the spread even more, registering values like 1.16 in December and 1.21 by late February). This strongly suggests higher values at resolution. Following instructions, confidence is highest for the near term and scaled toward 0.5 for more distant horizons.
